channel.title: Green Curve Insights
channel.description: Daily ESG and climate compliance intelligence for Indian businesses.

item.title: CPCB Tightens the Screws: What the Notice to E-Waste Recyclers Means for EPR Compliance
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued a notice directing registered e-waste recyclers to strictly comply with the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, signaling heightened scrutiny of the EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility) credit ecosystem. The move is a clear warning shot: recycler

item.title: CPCB's New EPR Certificate Framework for E-Waste: What Producers Need to Know About Metal-Based Targets
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has released a framework that fundamentally changes how Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) certificates are generated under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022 — moving from a unit/weight-based system to one anchored in actual metal recovery (Gold, Cop

item.title: CPCB Notice: Interim Arrangement for E-Waste EPR Credit Generation and Transfer by Recyclers
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued an interim arrangement governing how registered E-Waste Recyclers generate and transfer EPR end-product credits (certificates) on the EPR Portal. This is a transitional measure under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022, designed to keep the EPR

item.title: CPCB Interim Arrangement for Tyre Waste EPR Credits: What Recyclers and Producers Must Know
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued an interim arrangement governing how registered tyre waste recyclers generate and transfer Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) end product credits — known as EPR certificates — on the centralised online portal. The move is designed to bridge o

item.title: Food Systems Under the ESG Lens: What India's Agri-Business Boards Must Act On Before BRSR Core and TNFD Disclosures Converge
item.description: Food and agriculture sits at the exact intersection of nature risk, climate transition, and supply chain disclosure — three regulatory currents converging simultaneously on Indian listed companies. GreenBiz's sustained coverage of this sector (trellis.net/topic/food-agriculture/) reflects a global e

item.title: Candour as Capital: Why Honest Sustainability Reporting Drives Shareholder Value — and What Indian CFOs Must Learn Before BRSR Core Enforcement Arrives
item.description: New research published by GreenBiz (trellis.net) confirms what sophisticated ESG investors have long suspected: companies that disclose sustainability shortcomings with specificity and accountability generate measurable share price outperformance, while glossy, kitchen-sink sustainability reports ac

item.title: SBTi's 2026–2030 Strategic Reset: What India's BSE 500 Companies, SBTi Commiters, and ESG Investors Must Recalibrate Now
item.description: The Science Based Targets initiative (SBTi) has published a landmark 35-page Strategic Plan for 2026–2030 that fundamentally repositions the organisation from a target-setting body to a 'transformation partner' — shifting toward sector-specific guidance, softening enforcement when companies miss tar

item.title: Decarbonisation at an Inflection Point: What Indian Boards Must Read Into the Global Momentum Shift
item.description: Global decarbonisation discourse is accelerating beyond voluntary pledges into mandatory measurement, verified target-setting, and capital allocation consequences — and Indian listed companies, particularly those in carbon-intensive and export-linked sectors, are now squarely in the crosshairs of th

item.title: IFS Zero AI Emissions Platform: What Asset-Intensive Indian Industrials Must Understand About the GHG Data Infrastructure Race
item.description: IFS — a global industrial software provider — has launched IFS Zero, an agentic AI-powered emissions measurement and reporting platform targeting asset-intensive industries such as manufacturing, energy, and infrastructure. The platform automates Scope 1, 2, and 3 GHG data collection, validation, an

item.title: California SB 253's Audit-Grade Carbon Data Mandate: Why Indian CFOs and CSOs Must Act Now
item.description: California's SB 253 — the Climate Corporate Data Accountability Act — mandates that companies with over $1 billion in annual revenue doing business in California disclose Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions with independent third-party assurance, with Scope 1 and 2 reporting commencing August 2026 and Scope

item.title: Carbonfact–Vaayu Merger: Why Product-Level Carbon Data Is Now a Compliance Imperative for India's Apparel and Textile Exporters
item.description: The acquisition of Berlin-based Vaayu by Paris-based Carbonfact — reported by ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/carbonfact-acquires-fashion-sustainability-software-provider-vaayu/) — signals a pivotal consolidation in fashion sustainability infrastructure, creating a combined platform with 150,000

item.title: DigitalBridge–ArcLight Merger: What a $1 Billion AI-Power Infrastructure Convergence Deal Means for India's Data Centre Developers, Grid Investors, and BRSR Disclosers
item.description: DigitalBridge's $1.05 billion acquisition of ArcLight Capital Partners — reported by ESG Today (https://www.esgtoday.com/digitalbridge-acquires-electrification-focused-investor-arclight-for-1-billion/) — is not merely a US private markets transaction. It is a structural signal that global institutio

item.title: New York Rolls Back 2030 Climate Target to 2040: What U.S. Sub-National Climate Retreat Means for Indian Exporters, Carbon Markets, and BRSR Strategy
item.description: New York Governor Kathy Hochul has signed the state's FY2027 budget, formally amending the landmark 2019 Climate Leadership and Community Protection Act (CLCPA) to replace the state's 40% GHG reduction target by 2030 with a new 60% reduction target by 2040 — a decade-long deferral of enforceable cli

item.title: Caudal Energy's Tidal Tech Raises $5.6M: What Bio-Inspired Marine Energy Means for India's Baseload Renewable Gap and Blue Economy Strategy
item.description: UK-based Caudal Energy, an Oxford University spinout, has raised £4.3 million (~$5.6 million) in seed funding to commercialise bio-inspired oscillating-fin tidal stream technology — a direct alternative to turbine-based marine energy systems — targeting first commercial deployment in 2028. For India

item.title: Plastic Waste Management Rules, 2016 (Amended 2018): The Foundation of India's EPR Regime
item.description: The Plastic Waste Management (PWM) Rules, 2016, as amended in 2018, form the bedrock of India's plastic governance framework, introducing Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR), the polluter-pays principle, and source segregation obligations across the value chain. For any company that manufactures,

item.title: CPCB Greenlights Customs Release for 106 EEE Producers: What Importers Need to Know
item.description: On 31 August 2023, the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) issued a letter to Customs Authorities facilitating the release of imported consignments belonging to 106 producers of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) covered under India's Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR) framework. This

item.title: CPCB Letter to Customs: Easing Import Clearance for 106 EEE Producers under E-Waste EPR
item.description: On 30 June 2023, the Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) issued a communication to Customs Authorities concerning the release of imported consignments belonging to producers of the 106 categories of Electrical and Electronic Equipment (EEE) covered under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022. The

item.title: CPCB Tightens E-Waste EPR: GST-Linked E-Invoicing Now Mandatory for Recyclers and Producers
item.description: The Central Pollution Control Board (CPCB) has issued a directive requiring all e-waste recyclers and producers registered under the E-Waste (Management) Rules, 2022 to use GST-linked e-invoices for all sales and purchases of e-waste and recovered end-products. This is a critical anti-fraud measure

item.title: AI Cannot Set Your Sustainability Ambition: What Indian CFOs and CSOs Must Keep Under Human Control
item.description: A timely analysis by Amy Skoczlas Cole published in GreenBiz/Trellis (https://trellis.net/article/3-things-sustainability-cant-afford-outsource-ai/) identifies three irreplaceable human responsibilities in sustainability practice that AI cannot substitute: setting ambition levels, reading cross-syst

item.title: The 2026 Sustainability Profession Report: What Shrinking Budgets and CEO Dependency Mean for India's ESG Leadership Pipeline
item.description: GreenBiz's State of the Sustainability Profession 2026 report (https://trellis.net/article/state-of-the-sustainability-profession-2026/) surfaces a structural tension that Indian boardrooms can no longer treat as a Western problem: sustainability functions are under simultaneous pressure from tighte
